sourceMovie.title: The Frighteners
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Frighteners works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Voices
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Frighteners clicked for you, The Voices (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Frighteners clicked for you, Beetlejuice (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Frighteners?
faq.answer: Try The Voices, A Haunted House 2, Mater and the Ghostlight—each shares DNA with The Frighteners while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Frighteners?
faq.answer: The Frighteners (1996) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Frighteners worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
